---
title: "Stellar Education: Your Progress Dashboard"
jupyter: stellar-proj
format:
  dashboard:
    theme: flatly
    nav-buttons: []
    scrolling: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  daemon: false
  cache: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import plotly.express as px
import networkx as nx
from pathlib import Path
from IPython.display import HTML

import plotly.io as pio
pio.renderers.default = "plotly_mimetype"

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SECTION 1 ── LOAD DATA
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# -- Load real data  ------
# obs_df          = pd.read_csv('student_observations.csv')   # observation-level
# assign_df       = pd.read_csv('assignment_scores.csv')      # per-HW summary
# roster_df       = pd.read_csv('student_roster.csv')         # student metadata
# kc_node_df      = pd.read_csv('kc_node.csv')                # KC definitions
# kc_edge_df      = pd.read_csv('kc_edge.csv')                # KC prerequisite graph
# bkt_mastery_df  = pd.read_csv('bkt_mastery.csv')            # BKT output (optional)

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SECTION 2 ── SYNTHETIC DATA ------
#   obs_df:     student_id, assignment_id, primary_kc_id, score, max_score,
#               percent_score, item_type, new_kc_id
#   assign_df:  student_id, assignment_id, raw_score, max_score, percent_score,
#               num_observations
#   roster_df:  student_id, display_name, performance_band, prior_stats_exposure
#   kc_node_df: kc_id, kc_label, reporting_group, difficulty_prior, intro_class
#   kc_edge_df: source_kc_id, target_kc_id, relation, confidence
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

np.random.seed(42)

# ── KC nodes ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
kc_nodes_raw = [
    ("KC.U1.01.statistical_context",           "Statistical context",         1, 0.25),
    ("KC.U1.02.observational_unit_variable",   "Observational unit/variable", 1, 0.30),
    ("KC.U1.03.categorical_quantitative",      "Categorical vs quantitative", 1, 0.35),
    ("KC.U1.05.distribution_shape",            "Distribution shape",          2, 0.40),
    ("KC.U1.06.measures_center",               "Measures of center",          2, 0.45),
    ("KC.U1.10.measures_spread",               "Measures of spread",          2, 0.50),
    ("KC.U1.13.standard_deviation",            "Standard deviation",          3, 0.60),
    ("KC.U1.14.five_number_summary",           "Five-number summary",         3, 0.55),
    ("KC.U1.15.boxplots",                      "Boxplots",                    3, 0.60),
    ("KC.U1.16.outliers_iqr",                  "Outliers (IQR rule)",         3, 0.65),
    ("KC.U1.17.skewness",                      "Skewness",                    3, 0.55),
    ("KC.U1.19.comparing_distributions",       "Comparing distributions",     4, 0.70),
    ("KC.U1.20.normal_shape",                  "Normal dist. shape",          4, 0.65),
    ("KC.U1.22.z_scores",                      "Z-scores",                    4, 0.75),
    ("KC.U1.23.normal_percentiles",            "Normal percentiles",          4, 0.80),
]
kc_node_df = pd.DataFrame(kc_nodes_raw,
    columns=["kc_id", "kc_label", "intro_class", "difficulty_prior"])

# ── KC edges ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
edges_raw = [
    ("KC.U1.01.statistical_context",         "KC.U1.02.observational_unit_variable", "supports", 0.80),
    ("KC.U1.02.observational_unit_variable", "KC.U1.03.categorical_quantitative",    "supports", 0.85),
    ("KC.U1.03.categorical_quantitative",    "KC.U1.05.distribution_shape",          "supports", 0.80),
    ("KC.U1.05.distribution_shape",          "KC.U1.17.skewness",                    "supports", 0.85),
    ("KC.U1.06.measures_center",             "KC.U1.13.standard_deviation",          "supports", 0.90),
    ("KC.U1.06.measures_center",             "KC.U1.14.five_number_summary",         "supports", 0.85),
    ("KC.U1.10.measures_spread",             "KC.U1.13.standard_deviation",          "supports", 0.90),
    ("KC.U1.10.measures_spread",             "KC.U1.14.five_number_summary",         "supports", 0.80),
    ("KC.U1.13.standard_deviation",          "KC.U1.22.z_scores",                    "supports", 0.90),
    ("KC.U1.14.five_number_summary",         "KC.U1.15.boxplots",                    "supports", 0.90),
    ("KC.U1.15.boxplots",                    "KC.U1.16.outliers_iqr",                "supports", 0.85),
    ("KC.U1.15.boxplots",                    "KC.U1.19.comparing_distributions",     "supports", 0.80),
    ("KC.U1.17.skewness",                    "KC.U1.19.comparing_distributions",     "supports", 0.80),
    ("KC.U1.20.normal_shape",                "KC.U1.22.z_scores",                    "supports", 0.85),
    ("KC.U1.22.z_scores",                    "KC.U1.23.normal_percentiles",          "supports", 0.90),
]
kc_edge_df = pd.DataFrame(edges_raw,
    columns=["source_kc_id", "target_kc_id", "relation", "confidence"])

KC_IDS    = kc_node_df["kc_id"].tolist()
KC_LABELS = dict(zip(kc_node_df["kc_id"], kc_node_df["kc_label"]))

# ── Student roster ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
roster_raw = {
    "S001": ("Student 01", "Developing",  "None"),
    "S002": ("Student 02", "Proficient",  "Calculus"),
    "S003": ("Student 03", "Advanced",    "AP Stats"),
    "S008": ("Student 08", "Emerging",    "None"),
    "S015": ("Student 15", "Proficient",  "None"),
    "S020": ("Student 20", "Developing",  "None"),
}
roster_df = pd.DataFrame(
    [(sid, name, band, exp) for sid, (name, band, exp) in roster_raw.items()],
    columns=["student_id", "display_name", "performance_band", "prior_stats_exposure"]
)

# ── Assignment scores  (obs_df is simulated from these) ──────────────────────
assign_raw = {
    "S001": {"HW1": (12, 21), "HW2": (18, 24), "HW3": (9, 20)},
    "S002": {"HW1": (19, 21), "HW2": (22, 24), "HW3": (17, 20)},
    "S003": {"HW1": (21, 21), "HW2": (23, 24), "HW3": (19, 20)},
    "S008": {"HW1": (16, 21), "HW2": (14, 24), "HW3": (11, 20)},
    "S015": {"HW1": (18, 21), "HW2": (19, 24), "HW3": (15, 20)},
    "S020": {"HW1": (10, 21), "HW2": (8,  24), "HW3": (6,  20)},
}
assign_rows = []
for sid, hws in assign_raw.items():
    for hw, (raw, mx) in hws.items():
        assign_rows.append({
            "student_id": sid,
            "assignment_id": hw,
            "raw_score": raw,
            "max_score": mx,
            "percent_score": round(raw / mx * 100, 1),
            "num_observations": mx,
        })
assign_df = pd.DataFrame(assign_rows)

# ── BKT mastery probabilities per student per KC ──────────────────────────────
_profiles = {
    # Advanced student (S003)
    "S003": {kc: min(0.98, 0.78 + np.random.uniform(0.05, 0.20)) for kc in KC_IDS},
    # Proficient students (S002, S015)
    "S002": {kc: min(0.95, 0.60 + np.random.uniform(-0.10, 0.30)) for kc in KC_IDS},
    "S015": {kc: min(0.92, 0.55 + np.random.uniform(-0.05, 0.30)) for kc in KC_IDS},
    # Emerging / middle (S008)
    "S008": {kc: max(0.10, 0.38 + np.random.uniform(-0.15, 0.30)) for kc in KC_IDS},
    # Developing students (S001, S020)
    "S001": {kc: max(0.08, 0.25 + np.random.uniform(-0.10, 0.25)) for kc in KC_IDS},
    "S020": {kc: max(0.05, 0.15 + np.random.uniform(-0.05, 0.20)) for kc in KC_IDS},
}
# Pinning specific KC values to create interesting edge cases for the training agenda logic
_overrides = {
    "KC.U1.01.statistical_context":         {"S003":0.96, "S002":0.88, "S015":0.84, "S008":0.71, "S001":0.45, "S020":0.28},
    "KC.U1.02.observational_unit_variable": {"S003":0.95, "S002":0.85, "S015":0.82, "S008":0.65, "S001":0.38, "S020":0.22},
    "KC.U1.13.standard_deviation":          {"S003":0.91, "S002":0.76, "S015":0.70, "S008":0.30, "S001":0.15, "S020":0.09},
    "KC.U1.22.z_scores":                    {"S003":0.88, "S002":0.72, "S015":0.68, "S008":0.22, "S001":0.10, "S020":0.06},
    "KC.U1.23.normal_percentiles":          {"S003":0.85, "S002":0.69, "S015":0.64, "S008":0.16, "S001":0.08, "S020":0.04},
}
for kc, sid_vals in _overrides.items():
    for sid, val in sid_vals.items():
        _profiles[sid][kc] = val

bkt_mastery_df = pd.DataFrame([
    {"student_id": sid, "kc_id": kc, "p_mastered": round(p, 3)}
    for sid, kc_dict in _profiles.items()
    for kc, p in kc_dict.items()
])

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SECTION 3 ── STUDENT SELECTION
# Change STUDENT_ID to any student in our roster.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
STUDENT_ID       = "S008"  
MASTERY_THRESHOLD = 0.80

# Pull student data
student_name = roster_df.loc[roster_df.student_id == STUDENT_ID, "display_name"].values[0]
student_band = roster_df.loc[roster_df.student_id == STUDENT_ID, "performance_band"].values[0]
prior_exp    = roster_df.loc[roster_df.student_id == STUDENT_ID, "prior_stats_exposure"].values[0]

mastery_series = (
    bkt_mastery_df[bkt_mastery_df.student_id == STUDENT_ID]
    .set_index("kc_id")["p_mastered"]
)
mastery_dict = mastery_series.to_dict()

hw_df = assign_df[assign_df.student_id == STUDENT_ID].copy()
overall_accuracy = hw_df["percent_score"].mean() / 100

# KC classification
mastered_kcs   = [kc for kc, p in mastery_dict.items() if p >= MASTERY_THRESHOLD]
developing_kcs = [kc for kc, p in mastery_dict.items() if 0.50 <= p < MASTERY_THRESHOLD]
needs_work_kcs = [kc for kc, p in mastery_dict.items() if p < 0.50]

# Build KC graph
G = nx.DiGraph()
G.add_edges_from(zip(kc_edge_df.source_kc_id, kc_edge_df.target_kc_id))

# Blocked KCs: not mastered AND at least one unmastered prerequisite
blocked = [
    kc for kc in needs_work_kcs
    if any(mastery_dict.get(pr, 1.0) < MASTERY_THRESHOLD for pr in G.predecessors(kc))
]

# Performance band and color
BAND_COLORS = {
    "Advanced":   "#0F6E56",
    "Proficient": "#185FA5",
    "Emerging":   "#854F0B",
    "Developing": "#A32D2D",
}
BAND_UI_COLORS = {
    "Advanced":   "success",
    "Proficient": "primary",
    "Emerging":   "warning",
    "Developing": "danger",
}
band_color    = BAND_COLORS.get(student_band, "#555")
band_ui_color = BAND_UI_COLORS.get(student_band, "secondary")

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SECTION 4 TRAINING AGENDA LOGIC
# Produces 3 next-step cards tailored to the student's mastery tier.
# Steps are KC-graph-aware: we prioritise unblocking prerequisite chains.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def build_training_agenda(mastery_dict, G, kc_labels, threshold=0.80, n=3):
    """
    Return up to n action items.
    Priority order:
      1. Unblock: KCs that, once mastered, unlock the most downstream KCs
      2. Quick wins: KCs close to threshold (p ≥ 0.60) with no unmastered prereqs
      3. Foundational gaps: very low mastery KCs with no prerequisites
    Each item carries context for the student-facing card.
    """
    weak = {kc: p for kc, p in mastery_dict.items() if p < threshold}
    agenda = []

    def unmastered_prereqs(kc):
        return [pr for pr in G.predecessors(kc) if mastery_dict.get(pr, 0) < threshold]

    def mastered_prereqs(kc):
        return [pr for pr in G.predecessors(kc) if mastery_dict.get(pr, 0) >= threshold]

    def downstream_unlocks(kc, depth=3):
        """How many KCs become reachable once kc is mastered."""
        reachable = set()
        frontier = list(G.successors(kc))
        for _ in range(depth):
            next_f = []
            for node in frontier:
                if node not in reachable:
                    reachable.add(node)
                    next_f.extend(G.successors(node))
            frontier = next_f
        return [u for u in reachable if mastery_dict.get(u, 0) < threshold]

    # Category A: quick wins, close to mastery, prereqs done
    quick_wins = sorted(
        [(kc, p) for kc, p in weak.items()
         if p >= 0.60 and not unmastered_prereqs(kc)],
        key=lambda x: -x[1]
    )

    # Category B: unblock opportunities, mastering this frees many downstream KCs
    unblock_candidates = sorted(
        [(kc, p) for kc, p in weak.items() if not unmastered_prereqs(kc)],
        key=lambda x: -len(downstream_unlocks(x[0]))
    )

    # Category C: foundational gaps very low, no prereqs at all (root KCs)
    foundational = sorted(
        [(kc, p) for kc, p in weak.items() if not list(G.predecessors(kc))],
        key=lambda x: x[1]
    )

    seen = set()
    def add_item(kc, p, action_type, action_label, rationale):
        if kc not in seen:
            seen.add(kc)
            unlocks = downstream_unlocks(kc)
            agenda.append({
                "kc": kc,
                "name": kc_labels.get(kc, kc),
                "p_mastered": p,
                "action_type": action_type,   # "quickwin" | "unblock" | "foundation"
                "action_label": action_label,
                "rationale": rationale,
                "prereqs_mastered": [kc_labels.get(pr, pr) for pr in mastered_prereqs(kc)],
                "prereqs_missing":  [kc_labels.get(pr, pr) for pr in unmastered_prereqs(kc)],
                "unlocks": [kc_labels.get(u, u) for u in unlocks[:3]],  # show max 3
                "unlocks_count": len(unlocks),
            })

    # Fill agenda slots in priority order
    for kc, p in quick_wins:
        if len(agenda) >= n: break
        add_item(kc, p, "quickwin", "Almost there — push to mastery",
                 f"You're at {p:.0%} — just a few more practice problems to cross the 80% line.")

    for kc, p in unblock_candidates:
        if len(agenda) >= n: break
        n_unlocks = len(downstream_unlocks(kc))
        if n_unlocks > 0:
            add_item(kc, p, "unblock", f"Unlock {n_unlocks} more topic{'s' if n_unlocks>1 else ''}",
                     f"Mastering this opens the path to {n_unlocks} dependent topic(s) in the unit.")

    for kc, p in foundational:
        if len(agenda) >= n: break
        add_item(kc, p, "foundation", "Build your foundation",
                 "This is a core building block. Strengthening it will make every later topic easier.")

    # Fallback: just pick lowest mastery
    for kc, p in sorted(weak.items(), key=lambda x: x[1]):
        if len(agenda) >= n: break
        add_item(kc, p, "gap", "Address gap",
                 f"This skill is at {p:.0%} — consistent practice will move it forward.")

    return agenda

agenda = build_training_agenda(mastery_dict, G, KC_LABELS, MASTERY_THRESHOLD, n=3)

# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# SECTION 5 COLOUR CODES
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def mastery_color(p):
    if p >= MASTERY_THRESHOLD: return "#1D9E75"
    if p >= 0.50:              return "#EF9F27"
    return "#E24B4A"

ACTION_STYLES = {
    "quickwin":    ("#1D9E75", "#E1F5EE", "🎯 Almost there"),
    "unblock":     ("#185FA5", "#E6F1FB", "🔓 Key unlock"),
    "foundation":  ("#854F0B", "#FAEEDA", "🏗 Foundation"),
    "gap":         ("#A32D2D", "#FCEBEB", "⚠️ Gap"),
}
```

## Row — Student header {height="5%"}

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "KCs mastered"
dict(value=f"{len(mastered_kcs)} / {len(KC_IDS)}", icon="check-circle-fill", color="success")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Overall accuracy"
dict(value=f"{overall_accuracy:.0%}", icon="bar-chart-fill", color="primary")
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Performance band"
dict(value=student_band, icon="award-fill", color=band_ui_color)
```

```{python}
#| content: valuebox
#| title: "Blocked KCs"
dict(
    value=len(blocked),
    icon="lock-fill",
    color="danger" if len(blocked) > 2 else ("warning" if len(blocked) > 0 else "success")
)
```

## Row — KC mastery & scores {height="35%"}

### Column — KC mastery bar chart {width="58%"}

```{python}
#| title: "KC mastery: Your Current Status on every skill"

sorted_kcs = sorted(mastery_dict.items(), key=lambda x: x[1])
labels = [KC_LABELS.get(kc, kc) for kc, _ in sorted_kcs]
values = [round(p * 100, 1) for _, p in sorted_kcs]
colors = [mastery_color(p / 100) for p in values]

fig = go.Figure(go.Bar(
    x=values,
    y=labels,
    orientation='h',
    marker_color=colors,
    text=[f"{v}%" for v in values],
    textposition='outside',
    cliponaxis=False,
    hovertemplate="%{y}<br>Mastery: %{x:.1f}%<extra></extra>",
))
_ = fig.add_vline(
    x=80, line_dash='dash', line_color='#777',
    annotation_text='Mastery threshold (80%)',
    annotation_position='top right',
    annotation_font_size=10,
)
_ = fig.update_layout(
    margin=dict(l=10, r=70, t=10, b=30),
    xaxis=dict(range=[0, 115], title='P(mastered) %', showgrid=False, fixedrange=True),
    yaxis=dict(title='', automargin=True),
    autosize=True,          # ← KEY FIX: let Quarto control the height
    plot_bgcolor='white',
    showlegend=False,
    font=dict(size=11),
)

# ── FIX: return the figure object, never call fig.show() in a dashboard cell ─
HTML(fig.to_html(full_html=False, include_plotlyjs="cdn"))
```

### Column - HW scores & KC graph {width="42%"}

```{python}
#| title: "Homework Scores By Assignment"

hws    = hw_df["assignment_id"].tolist()
scores = hw_df["percent_score"].tolist()
colors_hw = [mastery_color(s / 100) for s in scores]

fig2 = go.Figure(go.Bar(
    x=hws,
    y=scores,
    marker_color=colors_hw,
    text=[f"{s:.0f}%" for s in scores],
    textposition='outside',
    hovertemplate="%{x}: %{y:.1f}%<extra></extra>",
))
_ = fig2.add_hline(y=80, line_dash='dot', line_color='#777',
               annotation_text='80%', annotation_position='top right',
               annotation_font_size=10)
_ = fig2.update_layout(
    margin=dict(l=10, r=30, t=10, b=30),
    yaxis=dict(range=[0, 115], title='Score %', showgrid=False, fixedrange=True),
    xaxis=dict(title=''),
    autosize=True,          # ← KEY FIX
    plot_bgcolor='white',
    showlegend=False,
    font=dict(size=11),
)

HTML(fig2.to_html(full_html=False, include_plotlyjs="cdn"))
```

```{python}
#| title: "KC Prerequisite Graph: Your Mastery at a Glance"

_pos = {
    "KC.U1.01.statistical_context":         (0, 4),
    "KC.U1.02.observational_unit_variable": (0, 3),
    "KC.U1.03.categorical_quantitative":    (0, 2),
    "KC.U1.05.distribution_shape":          (1, 4),
    "KC.U1.06.measures_center":             (1, 2),
    "KC.U1.10.measures_spread":             (1, 1),
    "KC.U1.13.standard_deviation":          (2, 1),
    "KC.U1.14.five_number_summary":         (2, 2),
    "KC.U1.15.boxplots":                    (3, 2),
    "KC.U1.16.outliers_iqr":               (4, 1),
    "KC.U1.17.skewness":                   (2, 4),
    "KC.U1.19.comparing_distributions":    (4, 3),
    "KC.U1.20.normal_shape":               (3, 5),
    "KC.U1.22.z_scores":                   (3, 0),
    "KC.U1.23.normal_percentiles":         (4, 0),
}

edge_x, edge_y = [], []
for src, tgt in zip(kc_edge_df.source_kc_id, kc_edge_df.target_kc_id):
    if src in _pos and tgt in _pos:
        x0, y0 = _pos[src]; x1, y1 = _pos[tgt]
        edge_x += [x0, x1, None]
        edge_y += [y0, y1, None]

valid_kcs   = [kc for kc in KC_IDS if kc in _pos]
node_x      = [_pos[kc][0] for kc in valid_kcs]
node_y      = [_pos[kc][1] for kc in valid_kcs]
node_colors = [mastery_color(mastery_dict.get(kc, 0)) for kc in valid_kcs]
node_hover  = [
    f"{KC_LABELS.get(kc, kc)}<br>Mastery: {mastery_dict.get(kc, 0):.0%}"
    for kc in valid_kcs
]
short_labels = [kc.split(".")[-1][:6] for kc in valid_kcs]

fig3 = go.Figure()
_ = fig3.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=edge_x, y=edge_y, mode='lines',
    line=dict(width=1.2, color='#CCCCCC'), hoverinfo='none'
))
_ = fig3.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=node_x, y=node_y, mode='markers+text',
    marker=dict(size=20, color=node_colors, line=dict(width=1.5, color='white')),
    text=short_labels,
    textfont=dict(size=8, color='white'),
    textposition='middle center',
    hovertext=node_hover,
    hoverinfo='text',
))
_ =fig3.update_layout(
    margin=dict(l=5, r=5, t=5, b=5),
    showlegend=False,
    xaxis=dict(showgrid=False, zeroline=False, showticklabels=False, fixedrange=True),
    yaxis=dict(showgrid=False, zeroline=False, showticklabels=False, fixedrange=True),
    autosize=True,          # ← KEY FIX
    plot_bgcolor='white',
    font=dict(size=10),
)

HTML(fig3.to_html(full_html=False, include_plotlyjs=False))
```

## Row — Training agenda {height="25%"}

### Column {width="100%"}

```{python}
#| title: "🌟 Your Personalized Next Steps & Training Agenda"

# ── Legend strip ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
legend_html = """
<div style="display:flex;gap:16px;margin-bottom:14px;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center">
  <span style="font-size:14px;color:#666;font-weight:500">Legend:</span>
  <span style="display:flex;align-items:center;gap:5px;font-size:13px">
    <span style="background:#E1F5EE;color:#1D9E75;border-radius:6px;padding:2px 8px;font-weight:500">🎯 Almost there</span>
    Near mastery, no blockers. You can finish strong.
  </span>
  <span style="display:flex;align-items:center;gap:5px;font-size:13px">
    <span style="background:#E6F1FB;color:#185FA5;border-radius:6px;padding:2px 8px;font-weight:500">🔓 Key unlock</span>
    Mastering this opens many later topics.
  </span>
  <span style="display:flex;align-items:center;gap:5px;font-size:13px">
    <span style="background:#FAEEDA;color:#854F0B;border-radius:6px;padding:2px 8px;font-weight:500">🏗 Foundation</span>
    Core skill; Everything in the future builds on this.
  </span>
</div>"""

# ── Agenda cards ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def tag(label, fg, bg, border):
    return (f'<span style="display:inline-block;background:{bg};color:{fg};'
            f'border:0.5px solid {border};border-radius:4px;font-size:13px;'
            f'padding:1px 6px;margin:1px 2px 0 0">{label}</span>')

cards_html = '<div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(3,1fr);gap:10px">'

for i, item in enumerate(agenda):
    atype  = item["action_type"]
    col, bg, badge = ACTION_STYLES.get(atype, ("#555", "#f5f5f5", item["action_label"]))

    # Mastery bar
    pct  = item["p_mastered"]
    barw = int(pct * 100)
    bar_color = mastery_color(pct)

    prereq_done_tags = "".join(
        tag(p, "#0F6E56", "#E1F5EE", "#9FE1CB")
        for p in item["prereqs_mastered"]
    ) or tag("None needed", "#888", "#f5f5f5", "#ddd")

    prereq_miss_tags = "".join(
        tag(p, "#A32D2D", "#FCEBEB", "#F7C1C1")
        for p in item["prereqs_missing"]
    )

    unlock_tags = "".join(
        tag(u, "#185FA5", "#E6F1FB", "#B5D4F4")
        for u in item["unlocks"]
    )
    if item["unlocks_count"] > 3:
        unlock_tags += tag(f"+{item['unlocks_count']-3} more", "#555", "#f0f0f0", "#ccc")

    # Concrete next-step bullets (adaptive to tier)
    pct_int = int(pct * 100)
    if pct_int >= 70:
        bullets = [
            "Review 2–3 practice problems where you made errors.",
            "Redo any homework items below 80% for this topic.",
            "Try one AP free-response question using this skill.",
        ]
    elif pct_int >= 40:
        bullets = [
            "Re-read the relevant textbook section or class notes.",
            "Complete at least 5 additional practice problems.",
            "Ask your teacher to clarify the part you find hardest.",
        ]
    else:
        bullets = [
            "Start with the prerequisite skills shown below.",
            "Watch a short explainer video for this concept.",
            "Work through the scaffolded examples in your textbook.",
        ]

    bullet_html = "".join(
        f'<li style="margin-bottom:2px">{b}</li>' for b in bullets
    )

    cards_html += f"""
    <div style="border:0.5px solid #ddd;border-top:3px solid {col};
                border-radius:0 0 10px 10px;
                padding:14px 16px;background:white;display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:8px">

      <!-- Step number + name -->
      <div style="display:flex;align-items:center;gap:8px">
        <span style="background:{bg};color:{col};border-radius:50%;
                     width:24px;height:24px;display:flex;align-items:center;
                     justify-content:center;font-size:14px;font-weight:600;
                     flex-shrink:0">{i+1}</span>
        <strong style="font-size:15px;line-height:1.3">{item['name']}</strong>
        <span style="margin-left:auto;background:{bg};color:{col};
                     border-radius:6px;font-size:12px;padding:2px 7px;
                     white-space:nowrap">{badge}</span>
      </div>

      <!-- Rationale -->
      <div style="font-size:14px;color:#555;line-height:1.5">{item['rationale']}</div>

      <!-- Mastery progress bar -->
      <div>
        <div style="display:flex;justify-content:space-between;font-size:13px;color:#777;margin-bottom:3px">
          <span>Current mastery</span>
          <strong style="color:{bar_color}">{pct:.0%}</strong>
        </div>
        <div style="background:#eee;border-radius:4px;height:6px;overflow:hidden">
          <div style="background:{bar_color};width:{barw}%;height:100%;border-radius:4px;
                      transition:width .4s ease"></div>
        </div>
        <div style="display:flex;justify-content:flex-end;font-size:13px;color:#bbb;margin-top:2px">
          Target: 80%
        </div>
      </div>

      <!-- Concrete next steps -->
      <div>
        <div style="font-size:13px;color:#888;font-weight:500;margin-bottom:4px">
          ✅ What to do next:
        </div>
        <ul style="font-size:14px;color:#444;margin:0;padding-left:16px;line-height:1.7">
          {bullet_html}
        </ul>
      </div>

      <!-- Prerequisites & unlocks -->
      <div style="border-top:0.5px solid #eee;padding-top:8px;display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:6px">
        <div>
          <span style="font-size:12px;color:#999;display:block;margin-bottom:2px">
            ✔ Prerequisites you've covered:
          </span>
          {prereq_done_tags}
        </div>
        {'<div><span style="font-size:12px;color:#999;display:block;margin-bottom:2px">⚠ Prerequisites still needed:</span>' + prereq_miss_tags + '</div>' if prereq_miss_tags else ''}
        {'<div><span style="font-size:12px;color:#999;display:block;margin-bottom:2px">🔓 Will unlock:</span>' + unlock_tags + '</div>' if unlock_tags else ''}
      </div>

    </div>"""

cards_html += "</div>"

HTML(legend_html + cards_html)
```
